As of the 2025-10-26 cutoff, the relevant Wikipedia list still showed the Human parainfluenza virus infection/Croup row with no vaccine entry, and the prompt states the most recent known status on 2025-10-16 was No. Recent literature is consistent with that: a March 2025 review says no effective HPIV vaccines have been developed, and a June 2024 PNAS paper says HPIV3 lacks an approved vaccine. There is some live pipeline activity, including HPIV3-related clinical-trial work, but that is far short of a near-certain switch in Wikipedia’s vaccine column over the next week to three months. Because the horizon gets longer, I move slightly upward toward 0.5, but only modestly: a new vaccine emergence and corresponding Wikipedia update by these dates remains unlikely.

Human bocavirus is an obscure pathogen with no approved vaccine and apparently no active human-use vaccine program near the cutoff. The Wikipedia list entry was still blank for vaccines in the 2025-10-01 revision, only weeks before the first resolution date. The one targeted search found direct HBoV sources indicating no vaccine available and no vaccine in development for human use, while the pro-vaccine evidence was limited to older preclinical concepts like VLP immunogenicity in mice. That makes a Wikipedia flip to 'Yes' within 1 week, 1 month, or even 3 months very unlikely, though I allow a slightly higher chance by January simply because of the longer horizon.

The resolution source itself showed no vaccine listed for Human ewingii ehrlichiosis as of the latest pre-cutoff Wikipedia revision I could inspect, and the prompt says the most recent known value as of 2025-10-16 was also No. Outside view: going from no listed vaccine to yes in 1 week, 1 month, or even 3 months for a rare tick-borne bacterial disease is extremely unlikely. That makes a near-term E. ewingii vaccine, plus Wikipedia recognition of it, very improbable. I allow a slightly higher probability by the farthest date only because there is more time for an update or borderline interpretation, but overall YES remains very unlikely.

As of the 2025-10-26 cutoff, Lilly King still appears to be the long-course 100m breaststroke world-record holder at 1:04.13. The strongest specific evidence is that the latest major long-course meet, the 2025 World Championships, was not especially close to the record: Anna Elendt won in 1:05.19, with the podium all well off 1:04.13, and King herself missed the final (search_0_summary). That suggests no swimmer was on immediate world-record pace in the most relevant recent long-course competition. Also, the main elite circuit in late October 2025 was the World Aquatics World Cup, which was short-course, so it did not create immediate long-course world-record opportunities (search_0_result_1, search_0_result_2). 

So the near-term survival odds are very high. I trim them down slightly across the three dates because more time always allows more chances for an unexpected long-course meet, a breakout swim, or a pending ratification/update to surface. But given the lack of close recent long-course times, I still think YES is the clear favorite even by 2026-01-24.

As of the 2025-10-26 Wikipedia revision, Ariarne Titmus is the listed long-course women's 200m freestyle world-record holder at 1:52.23. As of the Wikipedia revision available before the cutoff, Ascariasis was still listed with no vaccine entry. The additional search found only preclinical or early-stage Ascaris vaccine work (in silico design, putative targets, mouse protection) plus a review explicitly saying no effective vaccine for human trial was available. For a neglected helminth infection to move from that state to a Wikipedia-recognized developed vaccine within 1 week to 3 months would be very unusual. I therefore assign very low probabilities, rising only slightly with the longer horizon because there is a bit more time for a genuine breakthrough or for Wikipedia to recognize one.

The tracked Wikipedia row for 'Enterovirus infection' was still effectively 'No' at the cutoff, and the prompt says the most recent known value on 2025-10-16 was also No. The strongest pro-YES evidence is that vaccines already exist for the subtype EV-A71, including licensed products in China, so a Wikipedia editor could in principle decide the broader row should list a vaccine. But those EV-A71 vaccines have existed for years, while the row remained blank/No through October 2025, suggesting Wikipedia has not been treating subtype-specific EV-A71 vaccines as a vaccine for generic 'Enterovirus infection.' That makes a near-term flip mostly an editorial-reinterpretation event, not a likely new scientific/regulatory breakthrough. So I keep the odds low, but rising modestly over longer horizons because there is more time for an edit.

This looks very unlikely across all three dates. The key outside-view point is that first-ever vaccines for old, well-studied infectious diseases almost never appear out of nowhere within 1-3 months; they usually follow years of visible clinical development and regulatory progress. Direct evidence also points against near-term resolution: recent sources describe syphilis vaccine work as still pre-clinical in 2025, and 2024-2025 literature frames vaccination as an unmet need rather than an achieved technology. Since bejel and yaws are closely related treponemal diseases, a flip to Wikipedia 'Yes' would most plausibly come from a syphilis/treponemal vaccine breakthrough, but the available evidence suggests the field is not close enough for that before Jan 2026. I therefore keep the near-term probability at the floor for Nov 2, nudging only slightly higher for later dates to reflect more time and thus greater uncertainty.

Since bejel and yaws are closely related treponemal diseases, a flip to Wikipedia 'Yes' would most plausibly come from a syphilis/treponemal vaccine breakthrough, but the available evidence suggests the field is not close enough for that before Jan 2026. I therefore keep the near-term probability at the floor for Nov 2, nudging only slightly higher for later dates to reflect more time and thus greater uncertainty.

The most likely path to YES is not a brand-new scientific breakthrough, but a Wikipedia/editorial reinterpretation of 'pneumonia' in light of already existing pneumococcal vaccines. That keeps the near-term odds low. As of the cutoff-era evidence, the resolution page still treated pneumonia as having no vaccine despite longstanding pneumococcal vaccines and even newer PCV21 approval, which suggests the page's convention is stricter than consumer phrasing like 'pneumonia vaccine.' A change is possible over time, but absent a strong new trigger it remains unlikely by all three dates, only rising modestly with the longer horizon.

Base rate is extremely low: Rickettsialpox is a rare, generally benign, neglected zoonotic bacterial disease, and the latest known Wikipedia status before cutoff was 'No'. The evidence I found points to broad experimental rickettsial vaccine research, not a specific developed vaccine for Rickettsialpox. Clinical references focus on doxycycline treatment rather than vaccination, and one 2025 source explicitly says vaccines are not yet available. Over just 1 week, 1 month, and ~3 months, the main path to YES would be either an unexpected breakthrough or a Wikipedia classification/editorial change, both of which seem unlikely. I therefore stay near the floor, with only a slight increase for longer horizons to reflect greater uncertainty.

Fabiano Caruana’s Wikipedia/FIDE rating on the October 2025 list is 2789, so 1% higher is about 2817. For 2025-11-02 and 2025-11-25, Wikipedia would still effectively be reflecting the November 1 list, so he would need an immediate ~28-point jump from an already elite level. That is extremely hard. The historical context I found is strongly bearish on a rapid rise: even in a very active stretch, he played 18 classical games, won two tournaments, and still had a net Elo change of -1.6; and as recently as January 2025 he was only 2803. By 2026-01-24 there is more time for two monthly lists and a hot run, so the chance rises, but 2817 would still require performance materially above his recent range.

Wikipedia’s FIDE rankings page as of 2025-10-22 still showed the 1 October 2025 women’s list, with Leya Garifullina at 2453. A YES therefore likely requires a future Wikipedia-listed rating of about 2478 or higher. That is a sizable +25 Elo jump from an already high rating. The short 2025-11-02 horizon is especially tough because she would need both a very strong November rating list outcome and for Wikipedia to reflect it quickly. By 2025-11-25, the page is more likely to have updated to the November list, so the probability rises somewhat, but it is still mostly a question of whether she gained ~25 points in roughly one rating period. By 2026-01-24, she has multiple rating periods to get there, so the chance is meaningfully higher. I still keep it below 50% because +25 from 2453 is substantial, even though recent reporting shows she is capable of a +27 month.

Baseline is almost certainly #13 on 2025-10-26: the Wikipedia FIDE rankings page fetched at the cutoff still shows Anand #13 on the 1 October 2025 list. The key issue is interpretation. If the market is resolved by raw numeric rank value, then YES means future rank number is >= 13, i.e. Anand stays #13 or slips lower; that looks more likely than him improving to #12 or better, especially since he is a late-career, likely low-activity player and the people just below him are close enough to overtake him. I therefore lean YES. I keep the probabilities well below the raw-numeric case because the wording 'higher ranking' is semantically ambiguous in chess, and because the Nov 2 date depends on whether Wikipedia has updated to the November list yet. Uncertainty increases by January because more rating lists and more rival activity can intervene.

The key issue is that the resolution uses Wikipedia’s FIDE_rankings page, which as of 2025-10-22 still showed the official 1 October 2025 list with Gukesh at 2752. That makes the 2025-10-26 baseline most likely around 2752, implying a target around 2780; however, the question background also mentions 2794 as the latest known value, so there is some ambiguity and a harsher interpretation would require roughly 2822. Either way, the short-horizon dates are tough: by 2025-11-02 and probably still by 2025-11-25, Wikipedia is likely reflecting the November official list, leaving little or no time from 10/26 for Gukesh to gain ~28 points. Search evidence also points to weak recent form and an October 2025 official rating around 2751/2752, after losing 43 points over the prior year and falling out of the top 10. By 2026-01-24 there is enough time for a rebound through additional rated events, and Gukesh is young enough that a strong surge is plausible, but a 1% increase from an already elite rating is still an underdog outcome.

Base rate dominates here. Wei Yi was listed at 2754 on the October 2025 FIDE rankings page, so a YES likely requires roughly 2782 or better. For an already-established 2750 super-GM, a +28 Elo jump is a very large move. The one concrete major opportunity I found before the farthest date is the FIDE World Cup running from 2025-10-30 to 2025-11-27; that is too late to matter much for the November list that should govern both 2025-11-02 and 2025-11-25 on Wikipedia, though the later November date is slightly more likely to reflect the November list rather than a stale October page. By 2026-01-24, the January list could include a strong World Cup and other late-2025 results, so the chance rises, but it still looks clearly below 50% because the required gain is so large.

As of the cutoff, the known Wikipedia status for Prevotella infection was No, and the best direct evidence I found was only a 2023 immunoinformatics/in-silico multi-epitope vaccine construct for Prevotella intermedia rather than any human-ready or clinically advanced vaccine. For Wikipedia to flip to Yes within days or weeks would likely require a much more concrete development milestone than that, and I found no sign of clinical trials, approval, or even robust preclinical momentum. I therefore put the near-term probability at the floor-like low end, rising only slightly by January because longer horizons allow more room for new research or a Wikipedia interpretation change.

Because 2025-10-26 was a Sunday, the effective comparison level is the prior market close on 2025-10-24. For large-cap stocks, the chance of being above a recent close over 1 week to 3 months is usually near 50%. GNRC is a single-stock direction question over short horizons, so the outside view is near a coin flip. I used the required time-series output as the main anchor: it was mildly bearish (combined 0.44), but that estimate was based on stale sampled data ending in May and a threshold far above the actual comparison price near the mid-October close around 191.67, so I only partially trust it. The one additional context search found a likely Q3 earnings release on Oct. 29/30, which is crucial because the first resolution date (Nov. 2) effectively uses the Oct. 31 close. The fundamental/news evidence was mixed: some upside narrative (fair value above market, strong next-year EPS growth, backlog/data-center opportunity) but also clear downside risk (recent Q2 miss, residential solar weakness, outage-demand risk). With no strong pre-cutoff edge, I stay very close to 0.5. I leave Nov. 2 slightly below even because the technical prior leaned bearish and earnings-event reactions are hard to call. For the later dates I pull the forecast closer to 0.5 to reflect higher uncertainty, per instruction.

Baseline is the 2025-10-24 close (because 2025-10-26 is a Sunday), about $24.45. For a large-cap consumer staples stock over horizons from 1 week to 3 months, the outside-view probability of being above the current price is near 50%. The strongest evidence here is the price-history model: despite a mild recent downtrend, it estimates a 0.57 chance KHC is above the baseline by 2025-11-02. There is also a clear near-term catalyst: Q3 2025 earnings are scheduled for Oct. 29, before the first two resolution dates. Pre-cutoff context is mixed but slightly favorable: management has said its $600M reinvestment should show meaningful H2 2025 results, but there are also headwinds from SNAP exposure and ongoing reinvestment pressure. So I keep the first date only modestly above 50%. For later dates, uncertainty should rise and weekly stock moves are noisy, so I pull probabilities back toward 0.5. I leave Nov. 25 slightly above 0.5 because the same earnings/guidance catalyst could still support the stock if received well, but by Jan. 24 mean reversion and broader market noise dominate.

I anchor on a near-neutral base rate for a large-cap defensive stock being above its current price over 1 week to 3 months, then lean slightly upward because the required time-series tool estimated about a 0.56 chance of being higher by early November. Base rate for a liquid large-cap stock being above its current price at a future date is around 50%, but the key adjustment here is that the comparison level is effectively MU’s 2025-10-24 close of 218.8, a fresh local high after a very sharp rally. I anchor near 50% because PEG is a large-cap defensive utility and short-horizon stock moves are usually close to a coin flip. The required price-history tool gave only a weak/stale bearish prior (combined P=0.44 for being above a June reference level by Nov. 2), but that signal is not directly comparable to the unknown 2025-10-26 reference close and may be distorted by corporate actions/price regime changes. The one useful catalyst I found is the confirmed Nov. 4 earnings report. That keeps the first date (effectively the Oct. 31 close) mostly in pre-earnings noise, so I stay just under even at 0.49. For Nov. 25, I lean slightly above even because PEG had FY2025 EPS guidance of 3.94-4.06 and analysts were expecting about $1.00 Q3 EPS, so there is at least some chance of a supportive earnings reaction, but guidance was near consensus and outlook was mixed, so only 0.51. By Jan. 24, the longer horizon adds more market noise, so I revert fully to 0.50.
